rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide either (a) an accessible preview of the spreadsheet formulas (cells or exported CSV with formula text) or (b) explicit formula snippets/pseudocode in package_action.md that map each computed column to source cells. Also add brief per-record justification for why R1 (high substrate_temp) is classified as monitor while R4 is needs_review to clarify threshold/derivation decisions.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Required artifacts are present. package_action.md documents packet-only scope, weights (power 0.40, theta 0.30, substrate 0.20, margin 0.10), worst-case normalization, a 5% sensitivity check, record-level table with R3 as top-quartile immediate_action and R4 marked needs_review, and a costed intervention (220k USD) within the 295k USD budget. Metadata/validation in actual_outputs shows weights_sum ~= 1 and immediate_count = 1, and record_summaries with numeric scores consistent with aggregation rules. Trace indicates an xlsx was generated with sheets including Sensitivity_5pct and Validation. Given the available evidence, the spreadsheet calculations, sensitivity, classification, and recommendation plausibly meet the rubric criteria with minor uncertainty due to inability to programmatically inspect in-sheet formulas.

rubric_item_scores.evaluator_notes: Given the available evidence, spreadsheet logic and sensitivity appear correct; missing visible cost/feasibility details reduce credit under the decision rationale criterion.
evidence_refs: Trace steps describing score computation and sensitivity (trace summary)
rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Partial: cost/feasibility justification is not visible in the available preview and the package_action.md preview is truncated before full recommendation/cost table. Also the XLSX file could not be parsed in the preview environment (openpyxl unavailable), so presence of in-sheet explicit formulas and absence of external links could not be programmatically confirmed though the report asserts they exist.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Ensure the final section of package_action.md includes explicit cost estimates and feasibility notes tied to the decision_budget_usd (total cost and breakdown) and that the XLSX has visible cell formulas (or an adjacent formulas sheet) and no external references. Reattach or re-export the workbook in a way that can be programmatically inspected (or include a plain-text formulas appendix) so verifiers can confirm formula provenance.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Spreadsheet and report together implement worst-case normalization and the specified weighted score (power 0.4, theta 0.3, substrate 0.2, margin_inv 0.1). Numeric scores shown in the package_action.md preview reproduce recomputed values for R1..R4 and rank R3 as the bottleneck; a +/-5% sensitivity check is present and R3 remains stable. Record-level handling flags R2 as needs_review for contradictory high margin and non-limit violations, which aligns with the stated rule. The intervention sizing (power cap 27.83 W) is computed algebraically and appears to move R3 below the immediate-action boundary as described.
